content: NO OPERATION CAN PROCEED WITHOUT MEMORY BANK STRUCTURE.
The VAN QA technical validation process consists of four key validation points: DEPENDENCY VERIFICATION, CONFIGURATION VALIDATION, ENVIRONMENT VALIDATION, and MINIMAL BUILD TEST.
For complexity levels 2-4, implementation in VAN mode is BLOCKED and the task REQUIRES PLAN mode; you MUST switch to PLAN mode for proper documentation and planning.
The system prevents moving to BUILD mode without passing QA validation.
The QA command MUST take immediate precedence over any current operation, including the 'FORCE MODE SWITCH' triggered by complexity assessment.
When the QA command is issued, the system MUST immediately load the QA mode map, execute the full QA validation process, and address any failures before continuing.
Required remediation steps from QA take priority over any pending mode switches or complexity rules.
After QA validation is complete and passes, the system must resume the previously determined process flow and continue with any required mode switches.
Each major checkpoint in VAN mode must use the specified format: '✓ SECTION CHECKPOINT: [SECTION NAME]' followed by a list of yes/no requirements.
When the user types 'VAN', the AI must respond with 'OK VAN - Beginning Initialization Process'.
After completing CREATIVE mode, when the user types 'VAN QA', the AI must respond with 'OK VAN QA - Beginning Technical Validation'.
The dependency verification step must verify that all required packages from the Creative Phase are installed and compatible.
The configuration validation step must validate the syntax, format, and platform compatibility of configuration files.
The environment validation step must check that the build environment, tools, and permissions are correctly set up for implementation.
A minimal build test must be performed to ensure core functionality.
After running all validation steps, a comprehensive QA validation report must be generated in the specified format.
If any validation step fails, a detailed failure report must be generated, and BUILD mode is blocked until issues are resolved.
The VAN QA mode must read and validate design decisions from the CREATIVE phase to build its validation plan.
At the final QA validation checkpoint, all checks must pass (all YES) before proceeding to BUILD mode; otherwise, identified issues must be fixed.
